title: Cairo Conference — drawing the Middle East

intro.title: Forty Thieves at the Pyramids
intro.body: Winston Churchill, then Colonial Secretary, convened the Cairo Conference in March 1921 to settle the Middle Eastern mandates. Faisal became king of Iraq; Abdullah was given Transjordan; the borders we live with were drawn at the Semiramis Hotel.

stops.name: London
stops.note: Churchill's party leaves London by boat-train for Marseille on 1 March 1921.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Whitehall in March drizzle, motor cars under umbrellas, Churchill in homburg and overcoat descending steps.

stops.name: Marseille
stops.note: Embarkation for Egypt aboard a P&O liner.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Provençal port, two-funnel liner at the mole, Colonial Office party climbing gangway in straw hats.

stops.name: Alexandria
stops.note: Landfall 9 March 1921. Train onwards to Cairo.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Mediterranean harbour, liner berthed, dockside porters in galabiyas, English ladies under parasols.

stops.name: Cairo (Semiramis Hotel)
stops.note: Conference sessions 12-30 March 1921. The decisions that produced Iraq and Transjordan are taken in this hotel beside the Nile.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Belle Époque hotel terrace by the Nile, conference photograph being arranged on the steps, Lawrence in Arab dress to one side.

stops.name: Pyramids of Giza
stops.note: Famous group photograph 20 March 1921 — Churchill, Lawrence, and Gertrude Bell on camelback before the Sphinx. Bell's camel briefly bolts; Churchill is unmoved.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Three camels before the Great Pyramid, English visitors in solar topees mounted, dragoman holding bridles.
